MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, Tel. 415.898.1555, appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff;
Mirna J. Scheffy, Esq. of Womble Bond Dickinson, Tel. 415.765.6221, appearing in person for Defendant Westburne Supply, Inc.;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: Defendant Westburne Supply, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment.

The matter is contested. Argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Westburne Supply, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Opposition filed. Reply filed.

Plaintiff's evidentiary objections to Exhibits H and I in the Woods Declaration (relevant portions of the 1994 and 2023 Deposition Transcripts of Anthony Piazza) is sustained. Even if the testimony was considered, the Court does not find that Piazza's former testimony flatly contradicts Plaintiff's response to Special Interrogatory No. 1 enough to shift the burden. The evidence is not what Defendant purports it to be. Piazza's failure in 1994 to recall the names of suppliers of "asbestos containing products" does not establish that he lacks or lacked information regarding "products" D supplied to his job sites. With respect to the 2023 former testimony, Defendant failed to provide the Court with the question preceding the answer submitted as evidence. Accordingly, the Court can infer nothing from this evidence.

Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that Plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to Defendant. (See Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant chose not to depose Plaintiff's product-identification witness, Anthony Piazza. Defendant failed to address documents identified in Plaintiff's interrogatory responses naming various deposition testimonies in other cases or persons with knowledge identified in Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses, including Kevin Krausgrill, and Stewart Escalle. (Wood Decl., Exh. E [Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Special Interrogatories] at 4, 6, 9, 10.) Defendant fails to foreclose the possibility that Plaintiffs may yet prove asbestos exposure and causation attributed to Defendant.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Teckla Clay-Pratt, CSR #13125, Tel. 310.490.4905, Email: teklapratt@gmail.com. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)
